proposed_base_material_variables.grain_size: The quantitative measure of the average crystal grain dimensions (e.g., ASTM grain size number) within the base material, which influences grain boundary density and intergranular/localized corrosion susceptibility.

proposed_conditioned_material_variables.degree_of_cold_working: The quantitative amount of plastic deformation or cold reduction (e.g., percentage thickness reduction) applied to the material prior to testing, which alters dislocation density and affects pitting initiation.
proposed_conditioned_material_variables.surface_orientation: The specific geometric face or crystallographic direction exposed to the test electrolyte (e.g., rolled face/Walzflächen vs. cut cross-section/Schnittflächen), which can exhibit distinct localized corrosion behaviors due to microstructural anisotropy.

proposed_experiment_variables.repassivation_potential: The explicitly measured critical potential value (e.g., Up) at which actively growing pits arrest and the passive film is restored on the material surface.
proposed_experiment_variables.electrochemical_test_mode: The specific mode of electrochemical control applied during the experiment (e.g., potentiodynamic/potentiokinetic sweep, potentiostatic holding, or galvanostatic polarization) to induce and measure localized corrosion.
proposed_experiment_variables.galvanostatic_current_density: The specific, constant anodic current density applied to the working electrode during galvanostatic testing used to determine characteristic pitting or repassivation potentials.
